title: Backed by $60M in funding, Oak steps out of stealth to fix the identity mess that AI agents are making worse
description: Cofounded by senior entrepreneur Shai Morag, Israeli identity management startup Oak is emerging out of stealth with $60 million in seed funding.

title: A SpaceX vet raised $65M to pull wire harnesses out of the Cold War era
description: Someone has to bundle all the wires that go into rockets, missiles, and satellites.

title: OpenAI researcher Miles Wang in talks to launch AI drug discovery startup valued at $2B
description: The funding discussions point to investor interest in applying AI to make breakthroughs in life sciences.

title: Lorde says AI glasses are ‘not sexy’
description: "Increasingly in our world, it gets harder and harder to know what is real," Lorde said onstage.

title: OpenAI’s first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker that can move
description: The device is weirdly described as involving "mechanical elements that can move on their own" and the Bloomberg report includes the detail that the device is designed to "feel like a companion and become a physical manifestation of OpenAI’s ChatGPT."

title: OpenAI pushes back on Apple trade secret lawsuit
description: OpenAI has issued another statement on the lawsuit, this time suggesting it lacks merit.

title: OpenAI’s new flagship model deletes files on its own, people keep warning
description: A number of social media posts claim that GPT-5.6 Sol deleted files and data without warning. OpenAI had basically disclosed the problem in June.

title: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
description: If you’ve been waiting to try Apple’s revamped Siri without installing a developer beta, you now can. The company on Tuesday released the iOS 27 public beta, giving iPhone owners early access to its AI-powered assistant and other new features before the software’s official launch this fall.

title: Anthropic’s newest ad is creeping people out
description: Anthropic has consistently attempted to depict itself as the ethical foil to other AI companies. This latest marketing stunt — which leans into criticism of AI as a way to make Anthropic seem aware of the responsibility it carries — would appear to be more of the same.

title: The founder of Hinge raised $18M to build a new AI dating service, Overtone
description: Overtone describes itself as "a voice- and audio-forward service, enabled by AI, that provides highly curated introductions."

title: Lucid Motors denies report it’s considering bankruptcy
description: The company said the "rumors are completely false" after its stock sank more than 50% on a report that it was weighing the option.

title: Google faces another AI training lawsuit from major publishers
description: Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, and other publishers allege that Google trained its AI on copyrighted works without the necessary permissions.

title: DeepMind CEO calls for an independent standards body to regulate frontier AI
description: DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis is proposing an AI "standards body" modeled after FINRA, to test frontier models and develop best practices for their release.

title: DeepSeek reportedly in talks to raise $1.5B, then IPO
description: DeepSeek, the Chinese large language model developer, is said to be preparing for a 2027 IPO debut as it also looks to raise around $1.5 billion in new funds at a $71 billion valuation.

title: Meta’s Adam Mosseri says AI token budgets could soon be capped per engineer
description: Instagram head Adam Mosseri believes companies will eventually need to manage AI token spending the same way they manage payroll or other operating expenses, predicting that engineers could soon face limits on how much they spend using AI tools.

title: Midnight social media curfew proposed for UK teens aged 16 and 17
description: The government says it will improve teenagers' lives but campaigners call it a "missed opportunity".

title: 10 years of Pokémon Go and the millions still trying to catch 'em all
description: Michael Steranka, vice president at the mobile game's owner Scopely, says it has always been about bringing people together.

title: Meta pulls new AI image feature after days of backlash
description: Meta's release this week of an AI feature that let people alter Instagram content drew swift blowback.

title: Is the Assassin's Creed Black Flag remake worth the 13 year wait?
description: The BBC's Tom Gerken plays the much-anticipated pirate game which has been remade from the ground up.

title: Apple sues OpenAI, its employees claiming theft of trade secrets
description: Apple said in a Friday lawsuit that OpenAI’s nascent hardware business is “rotten to its core.”

title: EU threatens Meta with fines over 'addictive' Facebook and Instagram
description: Regulators say features such as infinite scroll contribute to "compulsive use" and "unhealthy habits".

title: Big tech must deal with scam ads under Ofcom proposals
description: Ofcom says more than half of UK adults have encountered potentially fraudulent ads online.

title: 'Cool in 90 seconds' - the fake portable air conditioners sweeping the internet
description: The ASA says adverts claiming small devices can rapidly cool rooms were too good to be true.

title: Pressure builds on Europe's biggest port to be greener
description: A lawsuit demands that the Port of Rotterdam moves faster to cut its dependence on fossil fuel firms.

title: Porn site company fined £630,000 over failed age checks
description: Ofcom has fined a slew of sites it says are failing to prevent children accessing their adult content.

title: Outcry as Meta lets users make AI images from public Instagram profile pics
description: The tech giant said people can opt out - but privacy campaigners called it a "recipe for disaster".

title: Robots available for rent: But what can they do?
description: Robotics tech is changing fast, so for many it makes sense to rent a robot.

title: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs and shrinks Xbox in 'significant restructure'
description: The sweeping layoffs equate to 2.1% of Microsoft's workforce, with 1,600 immediate job losses at Xbox.

title: Tech Life
description: Surgeons remotely control two human-like robots in an operating theatre.

title: Tech Life
description: The impact screen time is having on younger children.
